info.title: Segments
info.description: API for managing segments in your ActiveCampaign instance

tags.name: Match All
tags.description: Return all Contact id's that match the given Segment and provided Additional Criteria

post.summary: Create a Match All Request for a new Segment
post.description: Find all Contact Id's that match the given Segment. The provided segment will be saved with a 24 hour TTL. A new match-all request will be initiated. If results are not ready within 4 seconds, a response that lacks a result-set will be returned. Use the returned segmentId and runId to poll and check back later to see if the result-set is ready.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Comma delimited list of fields for which to sort the results. Optionally prepended with a dash sign to indicate descending order. Allowed fields: id, email, phone, first_name, last_name, full_name, cdate, udate, account_name, score, score<score-id>, field<custom-field-id>. The score<score-id> option will sort off of the value of a particular score. The field<custom-field-id> option will sort off of the value of a particular custom field.

parameters.name: instant
parameters.description: If present, API will return instantly with a runId without waiting for up to 4 seconds for the results to be ready. Useful if you want to kick off a match-all request but don't need the results immediately

200.description: Segment match all request started and/or completed

get.description: A new match-all request will be initiated. If results are not ready within 4 seconds, a response that lacks a result-set will be returned and the response's `is_ready` attribute will be equal to `false`. Use the returned segmentId and runId to poll and check back later to see if the result-set is ready

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Comma delimited list of fields for which to sort the results. Optionally prepended with a dash sign to indicate descending order. Allowed fields: id, email, phone, first_name, last_name, full_name, cdate, udate, account_name, score, score<score-id>. The score<score-id> option will sort off of the value of a particular score.

Examples: "email", "-phone", "score2", "last_name,-first_name"

parameters.name: instant
parameters.description: If present, API will return instantly with a runId without waiting for up to 4 seconds for the results to be ready. Useful if you want to kick off a match-all request but don't need the results immediately

200.description: Segment match all request started and/or completed

get.description:
If the result-set is not ready yet, the response's `is_ready` attribute will be equal to `false`. The match-all request has errored out if the `is_ready` attribute is false and the `run_id_end` attribute contains a valid timestamp`; when this occurs please try your request again or alert the support team. If either the RunId or the SegmentId have expired or otherwise do not exist, a 404 with error details will be returned.

result-sets are cached and returned in the same `sort` order that's requested. If the `sort` param is not provided it defaults to `id`. You can specify a different `sort` order than the original request, however this will break the result's cache for this `runId` and re-kick-off a new search for results using the same `runId`

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Comma delimited list of fields for which to sort the results. Optionally prepended with a dash sign to indicate descending order. Allowed fields: id, email, phone, first_name, last_name, full_name, cdate, udate, account_name, score, score<score-id>. The score<score-id> option will sort off of the value of a particular score.

Examples: "email", "-phone", "score2", "last_name,-first_name"

200.description: Segment match all request started and/or completed

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Comma delimited list of fields for which to sort the results. Optionally prepended with a dash sign to indicate descending order. Allowed fields: id, email, phone, first_name, last_name, full_name, cdate, udate, account_name, score, score<score-id>. The score<score-id> option will sort off of the value of a particular score.

Examples: "email", "-phone", "score2", "last_name,-first_name"

parameters.name: instant
parameters.description: If present, response will return instantly without waiting for the results to be ready

get.description:
If the result-set is not ready yet, the response's `is_ready` attribute will be equal to `false`. The match-all request has errored out if the `is_ready` attribute is false and the `run_id_end` attribute contains a valid timestamp`; when this occurs please try your request again or alert the support team. If either the RunId or the SegmentId have expired or otherwise do not exist, a 404 with error details will be returned.

Sorting is not supported for this endpoint. This endpoint can be used to pull results from a Match All or a Match Some request. The original sort value will be preserved and there is no risk kicking off a new request

200.description: Segment match all request started and/or completed

get.summary: Retrieve the most recent result count for the given segments that were ran without an AdditionalCriteria
get.description: One result per segment will be returned. Pagination not supported. Maximum 100 segmentIds per request. SegmentIds in the request that doesn't exist, will simply be ignored

parameters.name: segmentIds
parameters.description: A comma separated list of segmentId's. Example: id1,id2,id24,uuid-string,id5454

200.description: An array of segment count history results

get.summary: Retrieve all result counts for the given segment that were ran without an AdditionalCriteria
get.description: Up to 50 results will be returned at once. To get the next page, request again with the last result's timestamp formatted as an EPOCH seconds value

200.description: An array of segment count history results

get.summary: Retrieve all result counts for the given segment that were ran without an AdditionalCriteria
get.description: Up to 50 results will be returned at once. To get the next page, request again with the last result's timestamp formatted as an EPOCH seconds value

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Epoch seconds. Exclusive. Retrieves up to 50 segment count history records that occurred before this point in time

200.description: An array of segment count history results

external_id.description: Value to inject for any FieldDO's that have external set to `True`

limit.description: Maximum number of matches in the result-set

segment_relationship.description: Used to determine how the conditions should be conjoined with the segment conditions. Only required when OR'ing such that: `thisAdditionalCriteriaCondition or theSegmentConditions

AggFieldDO.description: Required if the aggregate objects is using the "most_recent" operator. This AggFieldDO defines the query logic that the most recent object must satisfy. When this field is present, the ConditionDO.fields attribute is used to specify the requirements prior to selecting the most recent record.
AggFieldDO.title: AggFieldDO

value.description: Value the `name` field's value should be evaluated against. Sometimes optional based on `operator`

name.description: Field whose value will be evaluated

AggregateDO.title: AggregateDO
AggregateDO.description: (Optional) AggregateDO allows for requiring a count of discrete matches against the simple (non-aggregate part of a) condition before the entire condition is considered satisfied.

aggregate_field_operator.description:
Compares the aggregate count of occurrences to the value of the `aggregate_field_value` field. 
Description of Values:
* "" - Entire AggregateDO object will be ignored
* "between" - Aggregate must be between `aggregate_field_start_value` and `aggregate_field_end_value`
* "most_recent" - Only the most recent record of the given SourceObject type will be looked at.
* "all" - All records of the given SourceObject type will considered.
* "" - aggregation is ignored "aggregate_most_recent_field" is required if "most_recent" is used. 
* All other values relate directly to only the aggregate_field_value field

aggregate_field_time_within_unit.description:
Look for aggregations within the aggregate_field_time_within_value with units equal to this value.
Not required if `aggregate_field_operator` is empty. If empty when `aggregate_field_operator` is not empty, "days" will be assumed.

aggregate_field_time_within_value.description:
Number of aggregate_field_time_within_unit to look for aggregations within. Not required if `aggregate_field_operator` is empty. When `aggregate_field_operator` is not empty, value must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to the maximum value for the unit specified, which are as follows:
* hours - 2160
* days - 90
* weeks - 13
* months - 3

fields.description: Defines the fields, values, types, and their operators. These act as search criteria for the Segment's condition.

hash.description: Sha1 Hash of the Condition's simple logic and its aggregation logic

hash_simple.description: Sha1 Hash of the Condition's simple logic

id.description: Numeric unique identifier for the Condition

object_type.description: Name of the Object being evaluated

operator.description: The operator that applies to the conditions in this group

values.description: A list of conditions in the group. All items in the "values" array must have the same "type"

id.description: Id of the Condition that belongs to this Condition Group. Conditions must not be in more than one group

type.description: Required. Type of "condition" indicates that the "id" attribute is a condition id.

value.description: Value the `name` field's value should be evaluated against. Sometimes optional based on `operator`

name.description: Field whose value will be evaluated

operator.description: All possible operators for evaluating the field's value against the `value` field

external.description: (Optional. Default False) If `external` is present and True, then the value of this FieldDO's "value" attribute is ignored and the externalId passed at-query time is used as the value of this FieldDO's "value" attribute. This attribute is commonly used to ensure the same pre-determined object satisfies multiple conditions

MatchAllAttributeDO.title: MatchAllAttributeDO

run_id.description: The runId of the match-all request. Use this value to poll for results or lookup results at a later point in time. RunId results can be viewed for 90 days

run_id_start.description: ISO 8601 datetime representing when the match-all request was made

run_id_end.description: ISO 8601 datetime representing when the match-all result-set was ready. If this date-time is present and `is_ready` is `false`, then the match-all request has ran into an error and will not finish; please try making a new match-all request or contacting support.

is_ready.description: True if the result-set is ready. False otherwise

matches.description: One page of the result-set of Contact Id's

segment_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime representing when segment was last created or modified. This timestamp can be used to look up the definition of the Segment at the time of the match-all request

MatchAllAttributeDO.description: Object representing the status and result of the match all request. Note that page number and total count information refer to the results in the "matches" attribute.

segment_condition_group_operator.description: The operator that combines the condition groups

user_id.description: The user that created this version of the segment

name.description: Optional. Segments with this attribute are Saved Segments and will show up on the /app/audiences page. Value must be unique. Can be defined at creation time, but cannot be added to a Segment during an update operation

id.description: Epoch timestamp the count was recorded at

SegmentCountHistoryAttributeDO.title: SegmentCountHistoryAttributeDO

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime representing when the when the result count wos recorded

segment_hash.description: A hash of the segment's logic. A changes in the hash over time indicate when the segment was modified

run_id.description: A unique value that identifies a segment's evaluation request

result_count.description: The number of matches found for this segment's evaluation request

active_result_count.description: The number of matches found for this segment's evaluation request AND are subscribed to at least one list

count_updating_with_runid.description: if the current count is being updated, this is the runid that update is waiting on

SegmentCountHistoryAttributeDO.description: Object that summarises the results of a segment's evaluation request
